safest_plan.entry_basis: Deeper green accumulation zone on the weekly chart (~0.05–0.065). The image shows this buy band below the current price; the text's 0.14 is Zero's existing average, not a fresh entry (estimated mid of band).

targets.basis: chart 'Avg entry' band ~0.14 — first reclaim level above current price (image)
chart resistance zone ~0.22–0.25 / Zero grade TP2 0.25
chart resistance zone ~0.30–0.33 / Zero grade TP3 0.32
chart 'Final target' green band ~0.55–0.60 / Zero grade TP5 0.58

safest_plan.stop_basis: Below the weekly accumulation zone (~0.05) (estimated). Zero stated no stop — it is a long-term spot hold, so there is no real structural invalidation; a weekly close under the bottom green band is the only structure break.

stance_by_source.summary: Long-term spot bag — convinced Dogecoin eventually reaches 0.58; averaged in at 0.14, currently underwater, and willing to add more at lower key levels.

conflicts: The 0.14 'entry' is Zero's existing average buy price, not a fresh level — price is now ~0.079, well below it, so buying at 0.14 would be chasing into a loss.
The weekly chart shows a deeper accumulation zone around 0.05–0.065 below current price that the text leaves out — a more honest place to add than 0.14.
Zero graded his own setup a fail (4/10) and gave no invalidation/stop because it is a long-term spot hold, so the higher targets are aspirational rather than a high-conviction trade.
plain_english: This is a long-term hold idea, not a quick trade. The trader behind it believes Dogecoin will eventually climb back toward 58 cents and has been buying, but his average buy price of about 14 cents sits well above today's price of roughly 8 cents, so he is currently losing money on it. The chart points to a cheaper buy area around 5 to 6.5 cents if the price keeps falling. Don't pay 14 cents — wait for a dip into that lower zone before adding a small amount, and treat the higher targets as a slow, long-term hope rather than a sure thing.
